steps.title: Identify the example-illustration pattern
steps.content: The first sentence describes demonstration sports generally; the second provides a specific example—glíma at 1912 Stockholm. One sentence exemplifying the other is the key signal.

steps.title: Match the example transition
steps.content:
”For example,” introduces a specific instance to illustrate a general claim—exactly what the second sentence does with glíma.
The other three all signal different logical relationships (simultaneity, future, contrast) that don't fit the example-introducing relationship.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
